claim: A three-layer folder/markdown routing system replaces complex agent architectures: Layer 1 primes global context (folder structure), Layer 2 loads workspace-specific context, Layer 3 supplies skills and tools.
actionable: Create a CLAUDE.md in your project root that maps your folder structure, workspace purposes, and naming conventions; reference it at the start of every AI session with 'read CLAUDE.md'.
source_title: Stop Building AI Agents. Use This Folder System Instead.

claim: Token limits force AI systems to avoid reading irrelevant files; separating work into multiple workspaces (writing, production, community) prevents token waste and improves context relevance.
actionable: Organize your project into 3-5 distinct workspaces; create a routing table in your CLAUDE.md that specifies which files to read for each task type.
source_title: Stop Building AI Agents. Use This Folder System Instead.

claim: Naming conventions (like 'api-guide-draft-v2.md') replace databases; AI can infer file locations and organize outputs automatically based on consistent naming patterns.
actionable: Define consistent naming rules for each content type (drafts: 'title-draft-vN', final: 'title-YYYY-MM-DD'); document these in your CLAUDE.md and watch AI organize outputs automatically.
source_title: Stop Building AI Agents. Use This Folder System Instead.

claim: A routing table in your context file (describing which files to read/skip for each task) eliminates token waste and prevents AI from reading irrelevant context or making unintended edits.
actionable: Add a task-routing table to your workspace CLAUDE.md: Task | Files to Read | Skip | Skills. Reference it explicitly when switching tasks.
source_title: Stop Building AI Agents. Use This Folder System Instead.

claim: Skills can be plugged into a folder workspace system via context; this is better than per-agent skill configuration because skills are only loaded when needed for specific workflows.
actionable: Instead of loading 10 skills globally, maintain a 'Skills' row in your routing table and conditionally load them only for specific task types in your CLAUDE.md.
source_title: Stop Building AI Agents. Use This Folder System Instead.

claim: Markdown files are more effective routing interfaces than visual UIs because AI parses English instructions better than interpreting UI states, and files are version-controllable and shareable.
actionable: Replace GUI-based agent configuration with markdown-based routing tables; commit them to git for version control and easy team sharing.
source_title: Stop Building AI Agents. Use This Folder System Instead.

claim: You can operate on a 22,000-line, 150-file production codebase using only folder structure and markdown routing; no specialized agents or frameworks are needed.
actionable: Apply the three-layer folder system to your production codebase: organize by feature/module, maintain a CLAUDE.md per workspace, and route context based on task.
source_title: Stop Building AI Agents. Use This Folder System Instead.

claim: The three-layer routing system is based on 50+ years of software engineering principles (transparency, composition, modularity) dating back to 1972; it's not a novel pattern.
actionable: Study classical software architecture (Unix philosophy, module systems, separation of concerns) to understand why this folder-based routing works; it's not AI-specific.
source_title: Stop Building AI Agents. Use This Folder System Instead.
